headline: two agents, same bug, one hour apart
sub: the second re-derived a fact the first never wrote down. one store fixed it. ~60% fewer tokens per lookup on my own fleet.

headline: one store. every read and write through it.
sub: no agent keeps a private copy of the truth to drift from. ~60% fewer tokens per lookup as a side effect.

headline: the model was fine. the source was stale.
sub: an agent cited a function i renamed in march. no error, just confidently wrong.

headline: most agent memory is just a second place for docs to go stale
sub: one current answer beats a big pile of remembered context.

headline: an agent pinged me about a spend spike
sub: two agents re-reading the same docs every task. routed through trovex: ~60% fewer tokens per lookup.

headline: a complete accuracy collapse beyond certain complexities
sub: frontier reasoning models, tested on controlled puzzles, past a problem-complexity threshold.
source: Apple Machine Learning Research, The Illusion of Thinking, NeurIPS 2025

cols.head: ROLLBACK PATH
cols.body: works without the agent in the loop.

cols.head: AUDIT TRAIL
cols.body: readable after the fact, by a human.

headline: headcount saved is the wrong number
sub: headcount saved gets cut the first bad quarter. these don't.
